status: PLAN — for review; no code written; no new economic decisions

tests.independent_derivation: flip one byte of registry/resolution/source in a tmp copy; assert REFUSED_CODE_OR_DATA_IDENTITY; unmodified chain loads.
drop each required field / wrong type / unknown configuration_id / eligibility not in {ELIGIBLE,INELIGIBLE}; assert fail-closed.
sigma in {missing,bool,str,inf,nan,0,-1} -> SIGMA_RESID_INVALID; supply inverse off by >1e-12 rel -> INVERSE_VOL_MISMATCH; within tol -> pass.
run identical candidate set under A/B/C; assert selection set differs only where |z| crosses 1.75/2.00/2.25 and every other rule/cost/exec/sample is byte-identical.
hand-compute w_i = (1/sigma_i)/sum_side(1/sigma) for a 3-name side; assert equal per side and sum==1 per side.
long gross == short gross == min(feasible long, feasible short, 0.5*NAV); hand-derive with asymmetric feasible sides.
one name targeted >1.5% NAV; assert capped to 1.5% and excess -> cash (no renorm).
a sector targeted >20% gross (or net >5%); assert reduced by removal cascade; hand-derive binding sector.
betas set so |Sum w_i beta_i|/gross > 0.10; assert removal until <=0.10; hand-derive numerator.
infeasible set with known |z|; assert the smallest-|z| candidate removed first; RemovalEvent evidence matches.
equal |z|; order by smaller signal_origin_session, then lexical permanent_security_id; assert deterministic.
after a removal, assert removed candidate NOT replaced by a lower-ranked one and remaining weights byte-identical (freed -> cash).
session with a pending exit + new entries; assert exit event ordering precedes entries; RC-3 state retained.
carry a pending exit across 2 no-open sessions; assert exactly one logical exit order (no duplicates).
attempt same-symbol entry while held or pending-exit; assert blocked (ineligible).
construction NAV != execution NAV within the order cycle -> REFUSED_CODE_OR_DATA_IDENTITY:NAV_IDENTITY_MISMATCH.
asymmetric ADV clips long vs short; assert both target and executed exposures recorded in ExposureSnapshot; clipped -> cash.
asymmetric clip shrinks gross so a sector ratio exceeds cap post-exec; assert INTEGRITY_STOP:REALIZED_SECTOR_CONSTRAINT and session REFUSED, state unchanged.
asymmetric clip worsens |Sum w beta|/gross > 0.10 post-exec; assert INTEGRITY_STOP:REALIZED_BETA_CONSTRAINT; no resize/substitute.
realized |net|/gross > 0.05 from clipping; assert a DriftRepairInstruction (next-open reduction, smallest |entry z| tie oldest->permanent id), session still commits; NO integrity stop.
realized gross==0; assert beta numerator 0, normalized_beta N_A_EMPTY_PORTFOLIO, beta passes vacuously, no inf/div0 serialized.
held symbol has no official open on session s; assert INTEGRITY_STOP:HELD_POSITION_OPEN_MARK_MISSING (no forward-fill/close/midpoint).
hand-derive NAV_s from 7-step valuation and r_s for a 2-session synthetic book.
assert first scoring return uses the preceding warm-up NAV, not a spurious zero-return first observation.
force a realized-constraint failure; assert cash/positions/pending/ledger/NAV/return-series byte-identical to the prior committed state; preview fills discarded; REFUSED replay retained as evidence.
run the full synthetic replay twice; assert byte-identical output_hash and self-hash verifies.
a -0.0 P&L/exposure serializes as -0x0.0p+0; a non-finite input refuses (CanonicalizationError / INTEGRITY_STOP).

static scan: no import of vendor/sealed adapters, evaluator_prototype, or real-data modules; assert report flags validation_data_read=oos_data_read=development_performance_computed=false, synthetic_fixture_only=true.

coverage_note: 28 tests covering identity, candidate validation, A/B/C parity, construction (normalization/neutrality/cascade/removal/ties/no-renorm), state (pending/dup/occupancy), execution (NAV identity/ADV asymmetry), realized fail-closed (sector/beta), net-drift repair, empty portfolio, NAV (missing mark/arithmetic/first-window), atomicity, determinism, signed-zero/non-finite, and the synthetic-only boundary. Every expected value is hand-derived or scipy/numpy-derived independently of the implementation.
